title: Resonant interactions in the $α$-FPUT lattice with site-dependent coefficients
authors: Lorenzo Migliorelli, Giovanni Dematteis, Sergio Chibbaro, Miguel Onorato
abstract: The wave turbulence framework has proven to be an effective tool for analyzing certain features of nonlinear energy transfer in one-dimensional nonlinear chains. In this work, we extend this approach to the $α$-FPUT problem when the spring stiffness $χ$ and the nonlinear coefficient $α$ are site-dependent. Although three-wave interactions are non-resonant for constant coefficients, their spatial modulation gives rise to a non-trivial resonant manifold. In this framework, we derive a new kinetic equation that suggests the possibility of substantially faster thermalization with respect to the constant coefficient case. The new kinetic equation includes also an extra term that can be associated to the Bragg-scattering mechanism, which promotes the isotropization of the wave-action spectral density function.
summary: This paper explores how spatial variations in the properties of a nonlinear 1D chain can accelerate the process of thermalization. By using wave turbulence theory, the authors demonstrate that site-dependent coefficients create new resonance channels that allow energy to redistribute much faster than in a uniform lattice.
main_problem: Investigating how spatial modulation of spring stiffness and nonlinear coefficients in an alpha-FPUT lattice affects energy transfer, resonant manifolds, and the thermalization timescale.
main_result: The derivation of a new wave kinetic equation shows that spatial modulation creates a non-trivial resonant manifold, leading to significantly faster thermalization (1/epsilon^2) compared to the standard case (1/epsilon^4) and introducing a Bragg-scattering mechanism.
method: Wave Turbulence theory using a perturbative treatment, normal form expansion (near-identity transformation), and Wick decomposition in the large-box limit.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional alpha-Fermi-Pasta-Ulam-Tsingou (alpha-FPUT) lattice with site-dependent spring stiffness and nonlinear coefficients.
key_observables: Wave-action spectral density function, spectral energy density evolution, and thermalization timescales.
important_parameters: Small nonlinearity parameter epsilon, site-dependent stiffness chi_j, and nonlinear coefficient alpha_j.
main_assumptions: Weak nonlinearity (epsilon << 1), weak spatial modulation of coefficients (order epsilon), and the large-box limit (L -> infinity).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the site-dependent alpha-FPUT model, applies Wave Turbulence theory and normal form expansion to derive a modified wave kinetic equation, analyzes the resulting resonant manifolds (including Bragg scattering), and discusses the implications for thermalization rates and the approach to the Rayleigh-Jeans equilibrium.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
